title: Fields Medal Winner Zimmermann Announces Joining OpenAI, Math's Highest Honor Recipient Shifts to AI Safety
excerpt: The International Congress of Mathematicians 2026 opened in Philadelphia, with the four recipients of this year's Fields Medal announced—Deng Yu, John Paden, Ja
content_html:
The International Congress of Mathematicians 2026 opened in Philadelphia, with the four recipients of this year's Fields Medal announced—Deng Yu, John Paden, Jacob Zimmermann, and Wang Hong. Among them, Deng Yu and Wang Hong became the first Chinese nationals to receive the Fields Medal since its establishment, while the newly crowned recipient Zimmermann announced at a press conference after the event that he would shift his research focus to AI safety and join OpenAI soon.

Core conjectures are solved, mathematical talent is highly praised by OpenAI

Zimmermann's key achievement leading to this award was redefining the theory of o-minimality as a fundamental research tool in arithmetic geometry and complex algebraic geometry, and successfully proving several core academic conjectures, including the algebraicity conjecture of Griffiths' period image and the André-Oort conjecture related to Siegel modular varieties, making a significant contribution to number theory development. Mark Chen, Chief Research Officer at OpenAI, and Babak, former Vice President of Microsoft AI and current OpenAI researcher, both confirmed and expressed their welcome. Mark Chen praised Zimmermann for his top-tier mathematical talent, and also admired his rigorous and cautious attitude towards AI safety issues, as well as his profound and insightful thinking.

*MO two-time gold medalist, studied under the renowned number theorist Sarnak

Zimmermann is a Canadian mathematician specializing in number theory, arithmetic geometry, and transcendental number theory, born in 1988. As a teenager, he represented Canada in the 2003 and 2004 International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO), winning two gold medals and achieving a perfect score in 2004. He studied at the University of Toronto, earned a Ph.D. in mathematics from Princeton University in 2011, and studied under the renowned number theorist Peter Sarnak. After that, he did postdoctoral research at the Harvard Society of Fellows, and has been teaching in the Department of Mathematics at the University of Toronto since 2014. A scholar who has just reached the peak of the mathematical world choosing to cross over into AI safety is itself a strong signal—that in the context of rapidly advancing AI capabilities, the appeal of AI safety governance is beginning to rival pure academic research for top talents.

sentences.zh: 一位刚刚登上数学界巅峰的学者选择跨界进入人工智能安全领域，本身就是一个强烈的信号——在人工智能能力快速提升的背景下，人工智能安全治理对于顶尖人才的吸引力开始与纯粹的学术研究相媲美。

content_html_zh:
2026年国际数学家大会在费城开幕，公布了今年菲尔兹奖的四位获得者——邓宇、约翰·帕登、雅各布·齐默尔曼和王宏。 其中，邓宇和王宏成为菲尔兹奖设立以来第一位获得菲尔兹奖的中国人，而新加冕的齐默尔曼在活动结束后的新闻发布会上宣布，他将把研究重点转向人工智能安全，并很快加入OpenAI。

核心猜想解决，数学人才获OpenAI盛赞

齐默尔曼获得该奖项的关键成就是重新定义了o-极小理论作为算术几何和复代数几何的基础研究工具，并成功证明了格里菲斯周期图像的代数性猜想和与西格尔模簇有关的安德烈-奥尔特猜想等多个核心学术猜想，为数论的发展做出了重大贡献。 OpenAI 首席研究官 Mark Chen 和前微软 AI 副总裁、现任 OpenAI 研究员 Babak 均予以确认并表示欢迎。 陈马克称赞齐默尔曼拥有顶级的数学天赋，也钦佩他对人工智能安全问题严谨谨慎的态度以及深刻洞察的思考。

*MO两届金牌得主，师从著名数论学家Sarnak

齐默尔曼（Zimmermann）是加拿大数学家，出生于1988年，专攻数论、算术几何和超越数论。 青少年时期，他代表加拿大参加了2003年和2004年国际数学奥林匹克竞赛（I​​MO），赢得了两枚金牌，并在2004年取得了满分。 他就读于多伦多大学，获得博士学位。 2011年获得普林斯顿大学数学博士学位，师从著名数论学家Peter Sarnak。 此后，他在哈佛大学研究员学会进行博士后研究，并自2014年起在多伦多大学数学系任教。 一位刚刚登上数学界巅峰的学者选择跨界进入人工智能安全领域，本身就是一个强烈的信号——在人工智能能力快速提升的背景下，人工智能安全治理对于顶尖人才的吸引力开始与纯粹的学术研究相媲美。
